channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: China sticks to economic growth target of 'around 5%' despite a looming trade war
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — The Chinese government unveiled an annual economic growth target of “around 5%” on Wednesday, despite the possible negative impact of a looming trade war with the United States, and pledged to address what it called “inadequate” consumer spending at home.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU and KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Elissa Slotkin assails Trump's early actions, offers Democrats a way to fight back
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — First-term Michigan Sen. Elissa Slotkin accused President Donald Trump of driving up costs while pushing for an “unprecedented giveaway to his billionaire friends” in Tuesday night's Democratic response to his first joint congressional address of his second term.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Powerful US storms kill 2 and bring threats from critical fire weather to blizzard conditions
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms killed two people in Mississippi, tore the roofs off an apartment building and a nursing home in a small town in Oklahoma and threatened more communities across the nation Tuesday with wide-ranging weather.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration releases list of hundreds of federal buildings targeted for potential sale
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration on Tuesday published a list of more than 440 federal properties it has identified to close or sell, including the FBI headquarters and the main Department of Justice building, after deeming them “not core to government operations.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Joann store closures: See the list of nearly 800 locations headed for sale
item.description: After more than 80 years, national fabric and craft retailer Joann is another step closer to being erased from shopping centers across the U.S. after the release of nearly 800 locations going up for sale in the coming months.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KTLA 5

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Lutnick: Trump may announce tariff compromise with Canada, Mexico on Wednesday
item.description: Trump implementing 25 percent tariffs triggered retaliatory measures from both nations, with major corporations also warning that it could lead to potential price increases for consumers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, ABC 4

item.title: The Florida attorney general's office is investigating Andrew and Tristan Tate
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Florida's attorney general announced Tuesday that his office has opened a criminal investigation into Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, days after they returned to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: IRS drafting plans to cut as much as half of its 90,000-person workforce, AP sources say
item.description: The IRS is drafting plans to cut its workforce by as much as half through a mix of layoffs, attrition and incentivized buyouts, according to two people familiar with the situation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Carolina man allegedly assaults family member over 2 meats cooked for dinner
item.description: A family member who witnessed the incident told police that the man was upset about two meats being cooked for dinner.
item.dc:creator: Jordan White, NewsNation

item.title: Florida man swallows $769K in stolen jewelry from Tiffany & Co.: police
item.description: A man was accused of stealing hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of jewelry from a Tiffany & Co. store in Orlando and swallowing the evidence.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 arrested after investigation leads to seizure of 44,000 fentanyl laced pills
item.description: BAKERSFIELD, Calif. (KGET) -- Two search warrants served by the Kern County Sheriff's Office on Monday resulted in multiple arrests due to alleged drug sales.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Eyraud, NewsNation

item.title: Restaurants owners who support tipped wage bill were doxxed: Dems
item.description: "We want to make it clear that these actions are unacceptable," Speaker Julie McCluskie and House Majority Leader Monica Duran said.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: IHOP offers free short stack for National Pancake Day
item.description: Calling all pancake lovers! If you are ready to satisfy your craving, IHOP is celebrating National Pancake Day on March 4 with a free treat.
item.dc:creator: Odyssey Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Jon Stewart accepts Elon Musk's demands for 'Daily Show' interview, fires back at his criticism
item.description: Jon Stewart is rejecting Elon Musk's criticism that he's a "propagandist," and added that he's "game" for an interview with the tech billionaire if he agrees to appear as a guest on "The Daily Show.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 9 states report measles cases as outbreaks grow
item.description: One of the most contagious viruses in the world is spreading around the United States. So far in 2025 there have been three distinct measles outbreaks and at least 164 cases reported to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention coming from nine states.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: 5 things to watch as Trump delivers joint address to Congress
item.description: President Trump will head to the Capitol on Tuesday for the first joint address of his second term emboldened by a month of mass federal agency slashing, shocking foreign policy moves and a dizzying array of executive actions that have disrupted Washington and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Accused Texas cop killer returns to court after Dem judge freed him on bond in ‘slap in the face’ move: expert
item.description: Dremone Francis, the Texas man accused of killing Deputy Fernando Esqueda in July 2024, is set to appear in court Tuesday for the first time since he was released on bond.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans target 4 'sanctuary' cities as Trump pushes mass deportations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans in Congress are taking aim at four cities — often called “sanctuary cities” — over their policies limiting cooperation with immigration enforcement with a hearing this week that comes as President Donald Trump presses ahead with his campaign of mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LA Times to give op-eds AI-generated political rating, list of alternate views
item.description: The Los Angeles Times has launched an AI-driven "bias meter" to provide readers with alternative perspectives on opinion content.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: FAA investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching DC airport
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) said Monday it is investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Trump trade war intensifies as tariffs go into place
item.description: Americans awoke on Tuesday morning to an intensifying trade war, as President Donald Trump’s newest tariffs on Canadian, Mexican and Chinese imports were imposed and those countries quickly moved to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Pope stable, but will sleep with ventilation mask, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis was in stable condition Tuesday and breathing with just the help of supplemental oxygen after respiratory crises a day earlier, but will resume using a ventilation mask at night, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US tariffs on Canada, Mexico take effect, as China takes aim at US farm exports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s long-threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico went into effect Tuesday, putting global markets on edge and setting up costly retaliations by the United States’ North American allies.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, PAUL WISEMAN and ROB GILLIES Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How South Carolina's execution of a condemned killer by firing squad will unfold
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — When a South Carolina man who killed his ex-girlfriend’s parents with a baseball bat steps into the death row chamber Friday night, it won’t be lethal injection or electrocution that ends his life.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: GOP rep introduces measure putting Trump face on $100 bill
item.description: First-term GOP Rep. Brandon Gill (Texas) has introduced a measure that would require all $100 bills to have President Trump’s face on them.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin audit of Trump win finds not a single voting machine error
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — An audit of the November election won by President Donald Trump in swing-state Wisconsin found that not a single vote was counted incorrectly, altered or missed by tabulating machines.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Foul! Cavaliers guard Ty Jerome blasts officials after whistle-happy crew calls 53 fouls
item.description: Upset with an inordinate number of personals called during Cleveland's 133-129 overtime win against Portland on Sunday, Jerome didn't hold back in criticizing the officiating crew — zeroing in on referee Natalie Sago.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Francis off mechanical ventilation entirely, signaling he's overcome risks from respiratory crisis
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in stable condition and didn’t need any mechanical ventilation Sunday, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Strip club Cinderella story 'Anora' wins best picture at the Oscars
item.description: "Anora,” a strip club Cinderella story without the fairy tale ending, was crowned best picture at the 97th Academy Awards on Sunday, handing Sean Baker’s gritty, Brooklyn-set screwball farce Hollywood’s top prize.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's next first speech to Congress is bound to have little resemblance to his last first one
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation will hear a new president sing a far different tune in his prime-time address before Congress on Tuesday night. Some Americans will lustily sing along. Others will plug their ears.
item.dc:creator: CALVIN WOODWARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Photos: The 2025 Oscars Red Carpet in Los Angeles
item.description: Hollywood's biggest stars and most talented artists are set to be honored Sunday evening during the 97th Academy Awards held at the Dolby Theatre in Hollywood. But before the awards ceremony itself begins, the stars are walking the iconic Red Carpet where fashion takes center stage.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: The 97th Academy Awards are underway
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The 97th Academy Awards have kicked off at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles. “Emilia Pérez” leads the pack with 13 Oscar nominations, but “Wicked” and “The Brutalist” are close behind. The 2025 Oscars opened with its biggest musical voices.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Oscars 2025: How to watch, and when the show begins
item.description: The Oscars themselves will start at 7 p.m. EST but red carpet coverage has already kicked off on ABC and Hulu, E!, and The Associated Press. The official Oscars preshow on ABC and Hulu will begin at 6:30 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Topsy-turvy Oscar season concludes today: Here’s what to look for at the show
item.description: Sunday’s Academy Awards will bring to a close one of the most unpredictable Oscar races in recent memory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: UK, France and Ukraine agree to work on ceasefire plan for Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: Britain, France and Ukraine have agreed to work on a ceasefire plan to present to the United States, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday as European leaders assembled in London to discuss ending the war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wyoming's only full-service clinic stops providing abortions after new regulations signed
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyol (AP) — Wyoming's only full-service abortion clinic stopped providing abortions Friday, but it remained open after Gov. Mark Gordon approved a law requiring that such facilities be licensed as surgical centers.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4
